snippet.title: Danny DeVito Interview on "Hoffa" (December 23, 1992)
snippet.description:
Daniel Michael DeVito Jr. (born November 17, 1944) is an American actor, comedian, and filmmaker. He gained prominence for his portrayal of the taxi dispatcher Louie De Palma in the television series Taxi (1978–1983), which won him a Golden Globe Award and an Emmy Award. He plays Frank Reynolds on the FX and FXX sitcom It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia (2006–present).

He is known for his film roles in One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest (1975), Terms of Endearment (1983), Romancing the Stone (1984), Throw Momma from the Train (1987), Twins (1988), The War of the Roses (1989), Batman Returns (1992), Jack the Bear (1993), Junior (1994), Get Shorty (1995), Matilda (1996), L.A. Confidential (1997), The Big Kahuna (1999), Big Fish (2003), Deck the Halls (2006), When in Rome (2010), Wiener-Dog (2016), and Jumanji: The Next Level (2019). He is also known for his voice roles in such films as Hercules (1997), The Lorax (2012), and Smallfoot (2018).

DeVito and Michael Shamberg founded Jersey Films. Soon afterwards, Stacey Sher became an equal partner. The production company is known for films such as Pulp Fiction, Garden State, and Freedom Writers. DeVito also owned Jersey Television, which produced the Comedy Central series Reno 911! DeVito and wife Rhea Perlman starred together in his 1996 film Matilda, based on Roald Dahl's children's novel. DeVito was also one of the producers nominated for an Academy Award for Best Picture for Erin Brockovich (2000).

In 2017, he earned a Tony Award nomination for Best Featured Actor in a Play for his performance in the Broadway revival of Arthur Miller's The Price.

snippet.title: Clive Anderson All Talk excerpt - interview with Danny Devito - BBC1 UK 26/12/1996 (VHS capture)
snippet.description:
An excerpt from the Clive Anderson All Talk show, aired on Boxing Day in 1996, in which he interviews Danny Devito to promote the UK release of the film "Matilda" (released before this programme aired, 20/12/1996).

Unfortunately the recording on this tape cut out part way through the interview, but thought that there was enough here to provide interesting viewing.

Uploaded for archival purposes only.

snippet.title: Michael Keaton's Thoughts on "Batman" After He Left | Late Night with Conan O’Brien
snippet.description:
(Original Air Date: 1/29/98) Michael Keaton discusses a nun battle at Catholic school, and his reaction to the "Batman" sequels.

FOLLOW CONAN ON SOCIAL
Conan O'Brien on Twitter https://twitter.com/ConanOBrien
Team Coco on Facebook https://www.facebook.com/teamcoco
Team Coco on Instagram  https://www.instagram.com/teamcoco/

ABOUT LATE NIGHT WITH CONAN O’BRIEN
Running from 1993-2009, Late Night with Conan O’Brien is one of the most beloved late night shows in television history. Known for its absurdist humor, hilarious celebrity interviews and featuring the best stand-up comedians and musical acts of the ‘90s and early 2000s. Hosted by Conan O’Brien alongside sidekick Andy Richter and house band Max Weinberg and the Max Weinberg 7, Late Night with Conan O’Brien featured unforgettable segments like “If They Mated”, “In the Year 2000”, “Desk Drive”, and made a breakout star of Triumph the Insult Comic Dog.

snippet.title: Danny Devito on Jack Nicholson Jimmy Hoffa & Tim Burton Batman - Later with Bob Costas12/30/92
snippet.description:
Actor and director DANNY DEVITO was the guest on the December 30th 1992 episode of LATER WITH BOB COSTAS. Devito was promoting the film "Hoffa" that he directed and starred in alongside JACK NICHOLSON. Much of the discussion centered upon Devitio's relationship with Nicholson. The role of Penguin in the Tim Burton film "Batman Returns" ended the interview. WKYC-NBC to cable to SuperBeta2 to Video8 LP to mp4 #dannydevito #jacknicholson #interview #bobcostas #batman #jimmyhoffa

Tiktok: https://www.tiktok.com/@gigacinema
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/gigacinema

Did you know that in Austin Powers in Goldmember… In the opening of the movie, Tom Cruise, Gwyneth Paltrow, Kevin Spacey, and Danny DeVito all make brief appearances. This is a reference to how I am praying to God to spare Danny DeVito from experiencing a significant controversy as the other three actors have.

snippet.title: Marc Maron's SHOCKING breakfast reveal!
snippet.description:
We made podcast history when Marc Maron revealed what he puts on his toast!

Watch this all-timer ep at youtube.com/watch?v=iMrstklTgEo OR get another hour with Marc PLUS much more with a free seven day trial at https://www.patreon.com/officehourslive.

Or become a member of this channel at https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCtS3BcCw-tITPFYSvkbP0Bg/join.

Office Hours Live is a weekly call-in show with Tim Heidecker, DJ Douggpound and Vic Berger (aka the holy trinity). Every episode is a wild audio-visual journey with drops, live music, and Tim's rants and riffs on life, music, politics, his kids and whatever else is happening in his life (and the lives of our callers). Special guests have included Jack Black, Fred Armisen, John C. Reilly, Chelsea Peretti, Weyes Blood, Reggie Watts, Bob Odenkirk, Thundercat and more.
